## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, I've got a great case study for you. This is a quick launch case study with Will Felkener. So Will's business is, he's a vet. And he teaches, especially his homeopathic medicine for animals. And he's built up an email list of approximately 8,000 people. So he took his existing product, which was an e-book. And it was an e-book and an audio book bundle that normally sells for $45. And this is just one of the offers he has. And what he did was he offered it at nearly a 50% off sale. And he also added in a couple of bonuses. We'll talk about that in a minute. But basically, this is a product that he says, some days he might have one or two sales. Some days he has no sales whatsoever. So it's not like he was selling very much of this. So it's not like he had a heck of a lot to lose by discounting it. So what he did was he added in a bonus special report and a one hour Q&A webinar. So he basically took this bundle that he already had for $45, wasn't hardly selling at all. And then he added in a special report, a one hour Q&A webinar, not too hard to do. And he dropped the price to $25. And he did this as a four day sale. His results were $8,845. Not bad. I mean, this is basically for the cost of, well, I guess he did put together a special report and he's gonna do a one hour Q&A webinar. Pretty easy to do to get on a webinar and answer questions for an hour. And he wrote six emails and he made almost $9,000. Now, another key thing here, it's been my experience over the years. There's two primary types of lists. There's a list of prospects, people that haven't bought from you yet, and a list of clients or customers who have bought from you. And in my experience, someone who has bought from you and that is on your client list is worth 15 times more on a per dollar basis than someone who hasn't bought from you. In other words, the next time you go to do a promotion for every subscriber that's on your client list and you compare that to a subscriber on your prospect list, your client list is gonna generate 15 times greater results. So you might have 1,000 person prospect list and 1,000 person client list. The prospect list might generate $1,000 in sales and that customer list will generate $15,000 in sales. And that has been my experience over and over in 20 years in business. And it varies on the offer and it can vary from business to business.

## [12:08 - 13:39]

When 50% of you likely missed the alerts. So basically this is a pivot. He just realized it wasn't a good day to close on Memorial Day, so he extended it one day and he gave his reason why. You can totally do this. You can totally do this. And this is the day, if we scroll way back up, this is the day where he did that extra 141 orders. So this was his original closing day. And then this was the day he sent that email. He said, duh, I screwed up. And so we got an additional 141 orders by doing it. So again, this is this reason why. And that's like a copywriting term. When you have this, what they call it, reason why copy, which is you tell people why you're doing something. And so the day before he said we're closing and then he came back and said, you know what, I screwed up. I should have closed on a holiday here in the US. And so I decided to extend it one more day. You can make the rules, you can totally do this. And then the final email is right here. This is just the last warning. You really need to on your closing day, and whatever launch you're doing, whether it's a seed launch, a quick launch, an internal launch, a JV launch, you always have to have at least two emails on the last day. And that's what he did here. And that's it. So this basically six emails. Six emails, he did almost $9,000 in sales, generated 555 new clients, and just did a fantastic quick launch.
